methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Oklo
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Nano Nuclear Energy, a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Jay Jiang Yu, commands a current valuation of $1.5B+ following $600M+ in total capital raised. The company operates within a peer universe of 11 comparable entities spanning public and private markets, including three public-traded peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private-stage counterparts such as TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Zap Energy.

Peer-median valuation analysis, the primary triangulation method applicable to this stage, is based on four same-sector comparables with similar valuation tiers. The peer set—TerraPower ($4.0B), X-Energy ($2.0B), Kairos Power ($1.6B), and Zap Energy ($1.0B)—yields a median valuation of $1.8B and a range spanning $1.0B to $4.0B. This peer-median method directly supports the $1.8B implied valuation.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $1.5B to $2.1B, with a median of $1.8B. This range anchors the current stated valuation at the lower bound while reflecting peer comparables at the midpoint. Primary uncertainty stems from the single-method foundation of this analysis; additional revenue multiple, discounted cash flow, or precedent transaction methodologies would strengthen conviction. The wide peer range ($1.0B to $4.0B) also signals material dispersion in how the market values early-stage nuclear ventures, reflecting unresolved commercialization and regulatory risk.
